name: Potential Sidecar Injection Into Running Deployment
notes:
Converted from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Leo Tsaousis (@laripping)
SIGMA Title: Potential Sidecar Injection Into Running Deployment
SIGMA Description: Detects attempts to inject a sidecar container into a running deployment.
A sidecar container is an additional container within a pod, that resides alongside the main container.
One way to add containers to running resources like Deployments/DeamonSets/StatefulSets, is via a "kubectl patch" operation.
By injecting a new container within a legitimate pod, an attacker can run their code and hide their activity, instead of running their own separated pod in the cluster.


False positives:
- Unknown

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event matches LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%patch%' AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%apps%' AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%deployments%' AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Potential Sidecar Injection Into Running Deployment on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event matches LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%patch%' AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%apps%' AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%deployments%' AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Potential Sidecar Injection Into Running Deployment
